mood: fragile refuge, organized desperation, rallying point
name: The Shingle — The Seawall
description: A bank of smooth, fist-sized stones rising four feet above the sand, offering the first real defilade from direct fire — men press against it in clusters, some reorganizing, some unable to move, some past moving.
atmosphericDetails: The stones are cold and wet and rattle underfoot with every near-miss, as if the earth itself is flinching.
Officers are moving along the shingle line trying to identify who is still functional — the faces are white under the helmet rims, eyes tracking the wire fifty yards ahead.
A bangalore torpedo section lies against the shingle wall, its carrying handle still attached, owner unknown.
